rag_common_patterns.overview: Reusable code patterns and implementations for RAG systems using IBM Watsonx and various vector database technologies.

pattern.name: multi_format_loader
ibm_cos_loader
pattern.description: Load documents from IBM Cloud Object Storage
pattern.code:
import ibm_boto3 from ibm_botocore.client import Config from langchain.document_loaders import PyPDFLoader import tempfile
def load_from_ibm_cos( bucket_name: str, prefix: str = "", cos_credentials: dict = None ) -> list: """Load documents from IBM COS bucket"""
# Initialize IBM COS client cos_client = ibm_boto3.client( 's3', ibm_api_key_id=cos_credentials['apikey'], ibm_service_instance_id=cos_credentials['resource_instance_id'], config=Config(signature_version='oauth'), endpoint_url=cos_credentials['endpoint_url'] )
documents = []
# List objects in bucket response = cos_client.list_objects_v2( Bucket=bucket_name, Prefix=prefix )
for obj in response.get('Contents', []): key = obj['Key']
# Download to temp file with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(delete=False, suffix=os.path.splitext(key)[1]) as tmp: cos_client.download_fileobj(bucket_name, key, tmp) tmp_path = tmp.name
try: # Load based on file type if key.endswith('.pdf'): loader = PyPDFLoader(tmp_path) elif key.endswith('.txt'): loader = TextLoader(tmp_path) else: continue
docs = loader.load()
# Add COS metadata for doc in docs: doc.metadata.update({ 'source': f"cos://{bucket_name}/{key}", 'bucket': bucket_name, 'key': key })
documents.extend(docs) finally: os.unlink(tmp_path)
return documents

pattern.name: semantic_chunking
pattern.description: Chunk documents based on semantic boundaries
pattern.code:
from langchain.text_splitter import RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter
def semantic_chunk_documents(documents: list, chunk_size: int = 400) -> list: """Chunk documents preserving semantic boundaries"""
# Define separators in order of preference separators = [ "\n\n\n", # Multiple blank lines (section breaks) "\n\n", # Paragraph breaks "\n", # Line breaks ". ", # Sentence endings "! ", # Exclamation sentences "? ", # Question sentences "; ", # Semicolons ", ", # Commas " ", # Spaces "" # Characters ]
splitter = RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter( chunk_size=chunk_size, chunk_overlap=int(chunk_size * 0.15), # 15% overlap separators=separators, length_function=len, is_separator_regex=False )
chunks = splitter.split_documents(documents)
# Add chunk metadata for i, chunk in enumerate(chunks): chunk.metadata['chunk_id'] = i chunk.metadata['chunk_size'] = len(chunk.page_content)
return chunks

pattern.name: code_aware_chunking
pattern.description: Chunk code files preserving function/class boundaries

pattern.name: milvus_collection_setup
pattern.description: Complete Milvus collection setup with IBM Watsonx embeddings
pattern.code:
from pymilvus import connections, Collection, CollectionSchema, FieldSchema, DataType from ibm_watsonx_ai import Embeddings from ibm_watsonx_ai.foundation_models.utils.enums import EmbeddingTypes
class MilvusRAGStore: def __init__(self, collection_name: str, embedding_dim: int = 768): self.collection_name = collection_name self.embedding_dim = embedding_dim
# Connect to Milvus connections.connect( alias="default", host=os.getenv("MILVUS_HOST"), port=os.getenv("MILVUS_PORT"), user=os.getenv("MILVUS_USER"), password=os.getenv("MILVUS_PASSWORD"), secure=True )
# Initialize IBM Watsonx embeddings self.embeddings = Embeddings( model_id=EmbeddingTypes.IBM_SLATE_125M_ENG, credentials={ "url": os.getenv("WATSONX_URL"), "apikey": os.getenv("WATSONX_API_KEY") }, project_id=os.getenv("WATSONX_PROJECT_ID") )
# Create or get collection self.collection = self._create_collection()
def _create_collection(self): """Create Milvus collection with schema""" fields = [ FieldSchema(name="id", dtype=DataType.INT64, is_primary=True, auto_id=True), FieldSchema(name="embedding", dtype=DataType.FLOAT_VECTOR, dim=self.embedding_dim), FieldSchema(name="text", dtype=DataType.VARCHAR, max_length=65535), FieldSchema(name="source", dtype=DataType.VARCHAR, max_length=1000), FieldSchema(name="metadata", dtype=DataType.JSON) ]
schema = CollectionSchema(fields, description="RAG knowledge base")
# Create collection if not exists if not Collection.exists(self.collection_name): collection = Collection(self.collection_name, schema)
# Create HNSW index index_params = { "metric_type": "COSINE", "index_type": "HNSW", "params": {"M": 16, "efConstruction": 256} } collection.create_index("embedding", index_params) else: collection = Collection(self.collection_name)
collection.load() return collection
def add_documents(self, documents: list): """Add documents to collection""" texts = [doc.page_content for doc in documents]
# Generate embeddings with IBM Watsonx vectors = self.embeddings.embed_documents(texts, batch_size=100)
# Prepare data data = [ vectors, texts, [doc.metadata.get('source', '') for doc in documents], [doc.metadata for doc in documents] ]
# Insert into Milvus self.collection.insert(data) self.collection.flush()
def search(self, query: str, top_k: int = 5, filter_expr: str = None): """Search collection""" query_vector = self.embeddings.embed_query(query)
search_params = { "metric_type": "COSINE", "params": {"ef": 100} }
results = self.collection.search( data=[query_vector], anns_field="embedding", param=search_params, limit=top_k, expr=filter_expr, output_fields=["text", "source", "metadata"] )
return results[0]

pattern.name: opensearch_hybrid_search
pattern.description: OpenSearch setup with hybrid search capabilities

pattern.name: complete_rag_pipeline
pattern.description: End-to-end RAG pipeline with IBM Watsonx
pattern.code:
from ibm_watsonx_ai.foundation_models import Model from ibm_watsonx_ai import Embeddings from ibm_watsonx_ai.foundation_models.utils.enums import EmbeddingTypes, ModelTypes
class RAGPipeline: def __init__(self, vector_store, model_id: str = ModelTypes.GRANITE_13B_CHAT_V2): self.vector_store = vector_store
# Initialize IBM Watsonx credentials credentials = { "url": os.getenv("WATSONX_URL"), "apikey": os.getenv("WATSONX_API_KEY") } project_id = os.getenv("WATSONX_PROJECT_ID")
# Initialize LLM self.llm = Model( model_id=model_id, credentials=credentials, project_id=project_id, params={ "decoding_method": "greedy", "max_new_tokens": 500, "temperature": 0.7, "top_p": 0.9 } )
def ingest(self, documents: list): """Ingest documents into RAG system""" # Chunk documents chunks = semantic_chunk_documents(documents)
# Add to vector store self.vector_store.add_documents(chunks)
return f"Ingested {len(chunks)} chunks from {len(documents)} documents"
def query(self, question: str, top_k: int = 5, include_sources: bool = True): """Query RAG system""" # Retrieve relevant documents results = self.vector_store.search(question, top_k=top_k)
# Format context context = "\n\n".join([ f"[Source: {doc['source']}]\n{doc['text']}" for doc in results ])
# Generate answer prompt = f""" You are a helpful assistant that answers questions based on the provided context.
Context: {context}
Question: {question}
Instructions: 1. Answer using ONLY information from the context 2. If insufficient information, say "I don't have enough information" 3. Cite sources using [Source: filename] 4. Be concise but complete
Answer: """
answer = self.llm.generate_text(prompt)
response = {"answer": answer}
if include_sources: response["sources"] = [ { "text": doc["text"][:200] + "...", "source": doc["source"], "score": doc["score"] } for doc in results ]
return response
def evaluate(self, test_questions: list, ground_truths: list): """Evaluate RAG system performance""" from ragas import evaluate from ragas.metrics import faithfulness, answer_relevancy
results = [] for question, truth in zip(test_questions, ground_truths): response = self.query(question, include_sources=False) results.append({ "question": question, "answer": response["answer"], "ground_truth": truth })
# Run RAGAS evaluation eval_results = evaluate( results, metrics=[faithfulness, answer_relevancy] )
return eval_results

pattern.name: conversational_rag
rag_ingestion_mcp_server
pattern.description: FastMCP server for RAG document ingestion
pattern.code:
from fastmcp import FastMCP from typing import Optional import os
mcp = FastMCP("RAG Ingestion Server")
# Global RAG pipeline instance rag_pipeline = None
def get_rag_pipeline(): """Get or create RAG pipeline instance""" global rag_pipeline if rag_pipeline is None: # Initialize vector store vector_store = MilvusRAGStore( collection_name=os.getenv("COLLECTION_NAME", "knowledge_base") ) rag_pipeline = RAGPipeline(vector_store) return rag_pipeline
@mcp.tool() def ingest_directory( directory_path: str, chunk_size: int = 400, chunk_overlap: int = 50 ) -> str: """ Ingest all documents from a directory into the RAG knowledge base.
Args: directory_path: Path to directory containing documents chunk_size: Size of text chunks (default: 400) chunk_overlap: Overlap between chunks (default: 50)
Returns: Status message with ingestion statistics """ try: pipeline = get_rag_pipeline()
# Load documents documents = load_documents(directory_path)
if not documents: return f"No documents found in {directory_path}"
# Ingest result = pipeline.ingest(documents)
return result except Exception as e: return f"Error ingesting documents: {str(e)}"
@mcp.tool() def ingest_from_ibm_cos( bucket_name: str, prefix: str = "", chunk_size: int = 400 ) -> str: """ Ingest documents from IBM Cloud Object Storage bucket.
Args: bucket_name: Name of IBM COS bucket prefix: Optional prefix to filter objects chunk_size: Size of text chunks (default: 400)
Returns: Status message with ingestion statistics """ try: pipeline = get_rag_pipeline()
# Load from IBM COS cos_credentials = { 'apikey': os.getenv('IBM_COS_API_KEY'), 'resource_instance_id': os.getenv('IBM_COS_RESOURCE_INSTANCE_ID'), 'endpoint_url': os.getenv('IBM_COS_ENDPOINT') }
documents = load_from_ibm_cos(bucket_name, prefix, cos_credentials)
if not documents: return f"No documents found in bucket {bucket_name}"
# Ingest result = pipeline.ingest(documents)
return result except Exception as e: return f"Error ingesting from IBM COS: {str(e)}"
@mcp.tool() def get_ingestion_status() -> dict: """ Get current status of the RAG knowledge base.
Returns: Dictionary with collection statistics """ try: pipeline = get_rag_pipeline()
# Get collection stats stats = pipeline.vector_store.collection.num_entities
return { "total_documents": stats, "collection_name": pipeline.vector_store.collection_name, "status": "ready" } except Exception as e: return {"error": str(e)}

pattern.name: rag_retrieval_mcp_server
pattern.description: FastMCP server for RAG retrieval and Q&A

pattern.name: multi_query_retrieval
pattern.description: Generate multiple query variations for better retrieval

pattern.name: self_query_retrieval
pattern.description: Extract metadata filters from natural language queries
pattern.code:
def self_query_retrieval(query: str, vector_store, llm, top_k: int = 5): """Extract filters from query and search"""
# Extract metadata filters filter_prompt = f""" Extract metadata filters from this query. Return as JSON.
Query: {query}
Available metadata fields: - doc_type: (policy, guide, faq, technical) - language: (en, es, fr, de) - date_range: (YYYY-MM-DD to YYYY-MM-DD) - author: (string)
Extracted filters (JSON): """
filters_json = llm.generate_text(filter_prompt, max_new_tokens=100)
try: import json filters = json.loads(filters_json) except: filters = {}
# Build filter expression filter_expr = None if filters: filter_expr = " AND ".join([ f"{key} == '{value}'" for key, value in filters.items() ])
# Search with filters results = vector_store.search( query, top_k=top_k, filter_expr=filter_expr )
return results

category.name: document_processing
category.item: Documents loaded from all supported formats
Appropriate chunk size for use case

category.name: vector_storage
category.item: Index parameters tuned for use case

optimization.name: batch_processing
optimization.description: Process documents in batches for efficiency
optimization.guideline: Insert into vector DB in batches

optimization.name: caching
optimization.guideline: Cache document embeddings to avoid regeneration
Use Redis or similar for distributed caching

optimization.name: index_tuning
optimization.guideline: Increase M for better accuracy (higher memory)
Tune ef_search based on latency requirements

security_practices.practice: Store credentials in environment variables or IBM Key Protect
Use IBM Guardium for data access monitoring
Implement role-based access control for knowledge bases
Encrypt data at rest in vector databases
Use TLS for all network communications
Audit all document access and queries
Implement rate limiting on API endpoints
